SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.controllerdevice

Class AntiSurgeController

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.process.controllerdevice.ControllerDeviceBaseClass
neqsim.process.controllerdevice.AntiSurgeController
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, ControllerDeviceInterface, ProcessElementInterface, NamedInterface
public class AntiSurgeController extends ControllerDeviceBaseClass
AntiSurgeController is a transient anti-surge regulator (P3) that reads the distance to surge from a Compressor and drives a recycle ThrottlingValve to keep the machine away from its surge line during trip, blow-down, start-up and load-rejection transients.

The controlled variable is the dimensionless surge margin returned by Compressor.getDistanceToSurge() (operating flow / surge flow - 1). When the margin falls below the configured set point the controller opens the recycle valve using a proportional-integral law; when the margin recovers the valve is closed again. The controller integrates the existing NeqSim transient controller framework (ProcessSystem.runTransient()) and adds no new compressor physics.

Output is the recycle valve opening in percent (0..100). The controller applies the opening directly to the recycle valve each transient step, so attaching it through valve.addController("anti-surge", controller) or compressor.addController("anti-surge", controller) is sufficient.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • AntiSurgeController

      public AntiSurgeController()
      Default constructor.

    • AntiSurgeController

      public AntiSurgeController(String name)
      Constructs an AntiSurgeController with the given name.
      Parameters:
      name - the controller name

    • AntiSurgeController

      public AntiSurgeController(String name, Compressor compressor, ThrottlingValve recycleValve)
      Constructs an AntiSurgeController wired to a compressor and recycle valve.
      Parameters:
      name - the controller name
      compressor - the compressor whose surge margin is protected
      recycleValve - the recycle valve actuator

  • Method Details

    • runTransient

      public void runTransient(double initResponse, double dt, UUID id)
      runTransient. Calculates controller output. Sets calc identifier UUID.

      If no engineering unit is configured, the controller falls back to the legacy percent-based error formulation used by earlier NeqSim versions.

      Reads the compressor distance to surge, computes the recycle valve opening with a proportional-integral anti-surge law (reverse acting: low margin opens the valve), clamps the output and applies it to the recycle valve.

      Specified by:
      runTransient in interface ControllerDeviceInterface
      Overrides:
      runTransient in class ControllerDeviceBaseClass
      Parameters:
      initResponse - Init value for response calculation
      dt - Delta time [s]
      id - Calculation identifier

    • updatePredictedMargin

      private void updatePredictedMargin(double previousMargin, double margin, double dt)
      Updates the filtered margin rate and predicted distance to surge.
      Parameters:
      previousMargin - the previous measured distance to surge
      margin - the current measured distance to surge
      dt - the transient time step in seconds

    • applyActuatorDynamics

      private double applyActuatorDynamics(double requestedOpening, double dt)
      Applies optional valve rate limiting and first-order actuator lag.
      Parameters:
      requestedOpening - requested valve opening in percent
      dt - the transient time step in seconds
      Returns:
      the actuator-limited valve opening in percent

    • applyToValve

      private void applyToValve()
      Apply the current valve opening to the recycle valve if one is configured.

    • clamp

      Returns:
      the clamped opening in percent

    • getPredictedMargin

      public double getPredictedMargin()
      Get the predicted distance to surge at the configured prediction horizon.
      Returns:
      the predicted distance to surge, or NaN before a finite margin has been read

    • getCompressor

      public Compressor getCompressor()
      Get the compressor being protected.
      Returns:
      the compressor, or null if none is set

    • setCompressor

      public void setCompressor(Compressor compressor)
      Set the compressor whose surge margin is protected.
      Parameters:
      compressor - the compressor

    • getRecycleValve

      public ThrottlingValve getRecycleValve()
      Get the recycle valve actuator.
      Returns:
      the recycle valve, or null if none is set

      public void setRecycleValve(ThrottlingValve recycleValve)
      Set the recycle valve actuator.

    • getSurgeMarginSetPoint

      public double getSurgeMarginSetPoint()
      Get the surge-margin set point.
      Returns:
      the surge-margin set point (dimensionless distance to surge)

    • setSurgeMarginSetPoint

      public void setSurgeMarginSetPoint(double surgeMarginSetPoint)
      Set the surge-margin set point. The recycle valve starts to open when the compressor distance to surge falls below this value.
      Parameters:
      surgeMarginSetPoint - the surge-margin set point (dimensionless, e.g. 0.10 for 10%)

    • getProportionalGain

      public double getProportionalGain()
      Get the proportional gain.
      Returns:
      the proportional gain in percent opening per unit margin error

    • setProportionalGain

      public void setProportionalGain(double proportionalGain)
      Set the proportional gain.
      Parameters:
      proportionalGain - the proportional gain in percent opening per unit margin error

    • getIntegralTime

      public double getIntegralTime()
      Get the integral time constant.
      Returns:
      the integral time constant in seconds

    • setIntegralTime

      public void setIntegralTime(double integralTime)
      Set the integral time constant.
      Parameters:
      integralTime - the integral time constant in seconds (0 disables integral action)

    • setOpeningRange

      public void setOpeningRange(double minOpening, double maxOpening)
      Set the allowable valve opening range.
      Parameters:
      minOpening - the minimum opening in percent
      maxOpening - the maximum opening in percent

    • setPredictiveActionEnabled

      public void setPredictiveActionEnabled(boolean predictiveActionEnabled)
      Enable or disable rate-aware predictive anti-surge supervision.
      Parameters:
      predictiveActionEnabled - true to use predicted distance to surge in the PI error

    • isPredictiveActionEnabled

      public boolean isPredictiveActionEnabled()
      Checks whether predictive anti-surge supervision is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if predictive supervision is enabled

    • setPredictionHorizon

      public void setPredictionHorizon(double predictionHorizon)
      Set the prediction horizon for rate-aware anti-surge supervision.
      Parameters:
      predictionHorizon - the prediction horizon in seconds; values less than or equal to zero disable prediction

    • getPredictionHorizon

      public double getPredictionHorizon()
      Get the prediction horizon.
      Returns:
      the prediction horizon in seconds

    • setMarginRateFilterTime

      public void setMarginRateFilterTime(double marginRateFilterTime)
      Set the margin-rate filter time constant.
      Parameters:
      marginRateFilterTime - the filter time constant in seconds; zero uses the raw finite-difference rate

    • setActuatorDynamics

      public void setActuatorDynamics(double valveRateLimit, double actuatorTimeConstant)
      Set actuator dynamics for the recycle valve command.
      Parameters:
      valveRateLimit - the maximum valve movement in percent opening per second
      actuatorTimeConstant - the first-order actuator time constant in seconds; zero disables lag

    • setEmergencyAction

      public void setEmergencyAction(double emergencyMargin, double emergencyOpening)
      Set the emergency fallback opening applied when the measured margin is very low.
      Parameters:
      emergencyMargin - the measured distance to surge that triggers emergency action
      emergencyOpening - the minimum valve opening in percent during emergency action

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Reset the controller integral state and output to fully closed.
